title: Demo quiz — Math & English (SEA classroom)
subtitle: Mock pack for pitching Lecture2Quiz SEA · tap an answer or press 1–4

questions.question: In North American English, what do people usually say for mathematics class?
questions.explain: Speakers in North America typically say “math”; British English often uses “maths”.

questions.question: What is the answer called when you **add** two numbers?
Which symbol is used for **multiplication** in “two times three equals six”?
Why might a lecture mixing English + a local language need transcription tuned for Southeast Asia?

options.A: SEA accents and code-switching need robust STT
options.B: Because MP4 files are always too small
options.C: Because quizzes never need bilingual summaries
options.D: Because HTTPS always fails in SEA

questions.explain: Real classrooms code-switch; platforms like Valsea focus on SEA speech patterns.

questions.question: Quick streak check: you’ve been on fire — what keeps good quiz UX?

options.A: Instant feedback + progress you can feel
options.B: No feedback until the end only

questions.explain: Immediate feedback and visible progress boost motivation (you’re proving it right now).
